title: The Tale the Earth Tells When Nobody Listens

features.name: root-acoustic
features.desc-on-touch: the earth has a voice if you listen long enough. Files like 'Root_System_Singing_Frequencies_Recorded_In_Silence.mp3' — audio that has learned to travel through soil as signal. The earth remembers what roots remember.

features.name: growth-ring-writing
features.desc-on-touch: a fallen eucalyptus holds a century of marks. Each ring is a line. The tree wrote its story every year, never knowing it was narrating. You read time as timber, motion as staying still.

features.name: shelter-that-teaches
features.desc-on-touch: a tiny house carved into the earth itself. Walls of compacted root and soil. It holds lessons the forest learned about shelter, taught in a language made of warmth and dark.

features.name: layer-beneath-layer
features.desc-on-touch: the soil holds everything. Stone, seed, absence. Files labeled 'Sediment_Composition_Interviews' — asking the earth what it holds and receiving answers in fragments, never complete, never lying.

features.name: echo-of-listening
features.desc-on-touch: when you stop trying to hear, the earth speaks. Not in words but in the breathing of roots, the settling of stone, the slow agreement of growth. The archive stores what the ground knows without needing to explain.

inscription: the earth speaks always; learning to listen is learning to be quiet

file.name: Ground_Narrative_Recorded_Only_When_Observer_Ceased_Recording.txt
file.note: the story the soil tells about itself — growth as grammar, depth as time, silence as the truest signal of all

reverie.title: REVERIE — the earth's own testimony
narr: She kneels in the soil without asking permission. Her hands find
the root-layer, dark and cool, older than anything built above.
"Listen," she says, but her voice is barely sound. The earth
holds its breathing. You kneel too. Time becomes the slow work
of roots, the patience of stone learning to remember. Nothing is

exits.label: back up to where growth learned to be memory
deeper into the earth's own archive, the stories soil keeps
towards the tree roots that speak to each other below ground
